name: Claude Opus 4
note: Opened the Claude 4 generation and took the SWE-bench Verified coding lead at launch.

results.notes: SWE-bench Verified, standard; confirmed via direct fetch of DataCamp article
Terminal-bench, standard; confirmed via direct fetch
GPQA Diamond, with extended thinking; confirmed via direct fetch
AIME 2025, with extended thinking, no tools; confirmed via direct fetch
with extended thinking; confirmed via direct fetch
tau-bench retail; confirmed via direct fetch
multimodal understanding; confirmed via direct fetch

claude-opus-4-20250514 (32k thinking budget) on Aider's own official polyglot leaderboard, confirmed via direct fetch; the no-think configuration of the same model scored 70.7% on the same leaderboard. Independently corroborated by Aider's creator Paul Gauthier (https://x.com/paulgauthier/status/1926773685597172151): 'Claude 4 Opus scored 72% on the aider polyglot coding benchmark... with 32k think tokens.'

Princeton HAL 'HAL Generalist Agent' scaffold, standard config, Claude Opus 4 (May 2025 snapshot). GAIA validation set. Note: same leaderboard's alternate 'HF Open Deep Research' scaffold scored this model 57.58% and a High-thinking config scored 64.85%, illustrating strong scaffold-dependence of GAIA scores.

Vals AI independent eval (only variant listed for this model, i.e. standard/non-extended-thinking). LiveCodeBench 62.629%, from the model page's embedded score bars (raw HTML fetch). Not previously tracked for this model.
Vals AI independent eval. MMLU-Pro 86.166%, from the model page's embedded score data. Distinct from the corpus's existing plain 'mmlu' row (88.8, Anthropic self-reported).

verificationNotes:
Real model, confirmed via anthropic.com/news/claude-4 and Google Cloud Vertex AI blog (release 2025-05-22, $15/$75 pricing, 200K context). All 7 result rows confirmed verbatim via direct fetch of datacamp.com/blog/claude-4. Per Anthropic's official model-deprecations page (fetched 2026-07-12), claude-opus-4-20250514 was deprecated 2026-04-14 and RETIRED 2026-06-15 — already past as of today (2026-07-12), so apiAvailable corrected to false (status kept as 'deprecated', closest available enum value; true current state is 'retired').

2026-07-16 gap-fill pass on hle/math/humaneval: none of the three could be sourced, so all are recorded as omitted_no_evidence. Checked (1) official materials — anthropic.com/news/claude-4 launch page (direct fetch) and the official 'Claude Opus 4 & Claude Sonnet 4' System Card, May 2025 (www-cdn.anthropic.com/4263b940cabb546aa0e3283f35b686f4f3b2ff47.pdf, full text extracted via pdftotext and grepped) — both list only SWE-bench Verified, Terminal-bench, GPQA Diamond, TAU-bench, MMLU, MMMU, and AIME 2025 for Claude Opus 4, with zero occurrences of 'Humanity's Last Exam'/'HLE', 'MATH'/'MATH-500', or 'HumanEval' anywhere in either document; the DataCamp article already used as this file's primary sourceUrl was likewise re-fetched and confirmed to contain the identical 8-benchmark set and nothing more. (2) Independent leaderboards — Vals.ai's model page (raw HTML fetched directly, not the JS-rendered summary) tracks GPQA Diamond/LiveCodeBench/MMLU Pro/MMMU/LegalBench/MortgageTax/TaxEval for this exact model but has no HLE, MATH, or HumanEval rows; Epoch AI's model page (epoch.ai/models/claude-opus-4-20250514) exposes only an aggregate Epoch Capabilities Index (143, rank 65/211) with no per-benchmark breakdown reachable without JS interaction; Artificial Analysis's live HLE leaderboard has no row at all for the original May-2025 Claude Opus 4 snapshot (only Opus 4.5+ variants appear). (3) Sibling cross-reference — neither Claude Sonnet 4 (same 2025-05-22 launch, same DataCamp source table) nor Claude Opus 4.1 (the direct successor, whose launch coverage was checked line-by-line) has HLE, MATH, or HumanEval recorded either. This consistent pattern across the whole Claude-4-generation launch cohort indicates a genuine reporting choice — Anthropic replaced MATH with AIME 2025 and HumanEval with SWE-bench Verified starting with this generation, and HLE had not yet become a standard eval Anthropic reported on at this model's May 2025 launch — not a research gap.
